folio.label: 138r
folio.l: And shamen al my kynrede , in this place
But I of hym , mygút gete bettre grace
But nathelees , I wyl of hym assaye
At certeyne dayes , ©ere by ©ere to paye
And thanke hi~ , of his grete curteisie
My trouthe wil I kepe , I wyl nougút lye
¢ Witú herte sore , he gootú vn to his coffre
And brougút gold , vn to his Philosophre
The value of fyue hundred pound , I gesse
And hi~ bisechetú , of his gentillesse
To graunte hi~ dayes , of the remenaunt
And seide Maister , I dar wel make auaunt
I failled neuäe , of my trouthe , as ©et
For sikerly my dette , shal be quyt
Towardes ©ow , how euäe that I fare
Xx To goon a begged , in my kirtel bare
But wolde ©e vouche sauf , vp on seuretee
Two ©ere or thre , for to respiten me
Thanne were I wel , for elles mot I selle
Myn heritage , ther nys na more to telle
This Philosophre , sobrely answerde
And seide thus , whan he theise wordes herde
Haue I nat holden , couenau~t vn to the
¢ ñis certes , wel and trewely , quod he
¢ Hast thow nat had thy lady , as the liketú
¢ No no q¶ he , and sorwefully he syketú
¢ What was the cause , telle me if thow can
¢ Aurelius his tale , a noon bygan
And told hi~ al , as ©e han herd bifore
It nedetú nat to ©ow , reherce it more
¢ He seide Arueragus , of gentillesse
Hadde leuere dye , in sorwe and in distresse
Than that his wyf , were of hir trouthe fals
The sorwe of Dorigene , he told hi~ als
How lotú hire was , to ben a wykked wyf
And that she lost had leuäe , that day , hir lyf
And that hir trouthe , she swor of Innocence
She neuäe erst , had herd speke , of apparence
That mad hi~ han of hire , so gret pitee
And rigút as freely , as he sent hir me
xxxx xx sent I hire , to hi~ a geyn
xxxx xx xxx xome , ther nys namore to seyn
xxxx xxxxxxxxxhre , answerd leue brother
xxxxxxx xx xxx , dide gentilly to other
